title: Rapid Entry By Jedi
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{MultipleIssues|citation|updatecontent=[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{Tactics |image= |name=Rapid Entry By Jedi |creator=[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] |date=[[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] |first= |last= |notable= |purpose=Search and seizure |requisites=[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] |utilized= }} {{Quote|You're going to put us out of business, sir. REBJ.''"
"''I wish that were true, but I suspect you're going to be busier than ever. REBJ?''"
"''Rapid Entry By Jedi, sir. Very handy. You'll be in demand.|A female Galactic Alliance Guard trooper and Colonel Jacen Solo|''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''}} '''Rapid Entry By Jedi''' was a term used to describe the expeditious, effective, and efficient methods by which a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was able to successfully conduct a search and seizure operation. '''"REBJ,"''' as it was colloquially abbreviated, was a catchphrase used by members of the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] in reference to a series of raids carried out by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the opening stages of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. ==Purpose== {{Quote|You want them immobilized?''"
"''That's the general idea, sir, so they don't detonate anything.|Colonel Solo and Sergeant Wirut|}} "Rapid Entry By [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]" denoted the harmless suppression of potentially dangerous [[Sentience/Legends|beings]] by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to incapacitate and subsequently detain them. The procedure it described, abbreviated to the acronym "REBJ," involved the extrication of the door of a barricaded location with minimal damage, and the harmless immobilization of suspected criminals or [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]], all through the power of the Force. ==History== {{Quote|Let's try a little REBJ. That's what you call it, isn't it? Rapid entry by Jedi? Okay, here goes rapid…|Colonel Solo, as he prepares to perform an "REBJ"|}} [[File:Darth_Caedus.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Colonel Jacen Solo of the Galactic Alliance Guard, and performer of "Rapid Entry By Jedi"]] "REBJs" were performed following a series of terrorist attacks on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] staged by [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] embittered by the rapidly deteriorating relationship between [[Corellia/Legends|their homeworld]] and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. Raids in Coruscant's [[Corellian Quarter]] were enacted by the Alliance's secret police, the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]], with one such blitz being carried out in [[Jabi Town]], led by two Jedi: [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]], [[Ben Skywalker]]. They approached a residence with locked doors, where their scanning equipment identified three individuals inside the premises equipped with heavy ordnance. [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Wirut]] determined the hostile and uncooperative nature of the occupants and motioned Solo to stay back as he prepared to enter the building and round them up. Solo, however, insisted on assisting, and after confirming with Wirut that abeyance was the desired outcome, used the Force to send a paralyzing jolt into the spinal cords of all three offenders. He then [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically]] blew the locked doors open, thus allowing members of the Guard to enter unabated and apprehend the unconscious criminals. One of the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]], jokingly remarked to Solo about how his efficient search and seizure tactics would soon put the Guard out of business. She then coined the phrase "REBJ," and when Solo inquired as to its meaning, the female trooper replied, "Rapid Entry By Jedi." Solo himself later used the term during another raid conducted by the Galactic Alliance Guard in Coruscant's [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|lower levels]]. During that encounter, Solo used the Force to gain entry into the location, and to remove the [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] from the assailants' hands. He then telekinetically slammed one of them, a [[Ailyn Vel|female]], against the wall and pinned her there until the members of his [[Squad/Legends|squad]] were able to arrest both individuals. ==Behind the scenes== The term "Rapid Entry By Jedi" was only used in the second of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novels, [[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines|''Bloodlines'']], by author [[Karen Traviss]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Combat tactics]] [[Category:Phrases]]
